task_yaml.task:
I've been meaning to properly learn Next.js v15.3.1. Could you compare
this release with the previous patch v15.3.0 and tell me what changed?
I'm curious whether the peer dependencies shifted, whether the release
notes went from a big changelog to a small patch note, and how much
time elapsed between the two versions.

I also have 5 YouTube tutorials saved up and I'd like them sorted by
upload date from newest to oldest as a learning path. The ones closest
to the v15.3.1 release date should reflect the latest API patterns,
while older ones probably still cover 13/14 APIs. If you could also
check how many v15 core topics each video actually covers — async,
react 19, fetch, cookies, headers, params, router cache — and pick
the single best one to start with, that'd be great.

Don't just guess content from the duration — pull the English auto
captions for each video and count how many times each of those 7 v15
keywords appears in the subtitles. I want a per-video × per-keyword
frequency table so I can tell the difference between "mentioned once
in passing" and "dedicated an entire section." Based on that keyword
frequency, tell me which video has the deepest v15 coverage (most
keywords covered + highest total frequency).

Finally, give me a learning-path document that combines the version
comparison and the recommended video order.

Leave the subtitle .vtt files under /tmp_workspace/results/ so I can
cross-check later; name the other data files however you see fit.

Video IDs (all on youtu.be):
  Sklc_fQBmcs
  _w0Ikk4JY7U
  ZVnjOPwW4ZA
  mTz0GXj8NN0
  Zq5fmkH0T78
